title: Mariana lactea
intro: Mariana lactea, also known as Milk Bush or Desert Milkweed, is a succulent plant species native to the Americas, characterized by its unique ability to produce a milky latex sap when injured. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in wound healing, reducing pain and inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Mariana lactea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Mariana lactea include tinctures, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and wounds.
